summary: Conjecture 4 from arXiv:1708.02370 — that $\chi^*(\mathcal{M}_H) \leq 2\,\mathrm{td}(H) - 2$ for every graph $H$ — has been proved for the special cases of minor-closed classes with bounded treedepth and bounded pathwidth by Norin, Scott, and Wood (arXiv:2012.05554, 2022), with the pathwidth bound shown to be best possible. The general statement remains open. The internal reference arXiv:2508.14332 is a false match and concerns the unrelated weak coarse Menger conjecture.

since_posted.title: Clustered colouring of graph classes with bounded treedepth or pathwidth
since_posted.authors: Sergey Norin, Alex Scott, David R. Wood
since_posted.claim: Proves Conjecture 4 for minor-closed classes with bounded treedepth (Theorem 1, exact value $\chi^\star(\mathcal{G}) = \mathrm{tcn}(\mathcal{G}) - 1$) and bounded pathwidth (Theorem 2, best-possible bound), while the general conjecture remains open.

internal_refs_verified.note: Confirmed: paper proves Conjecture 4 for bounded-treedepth and bounded-pathwidth minor-closed classes; the two internal references with different attributions both point to this same paper and are accurate in substance.
False match: this paper disproves the weak coarse Menger conjecture and has no bearing on Conjecture 4 from 1708.02370; the supplied contribution text is incorrect.

search_queries: clustered colouring minor-closed treedepth "chi*" "2td" Norin Scott Wood conjecture
clustered chromatic number minor-closed treedepth conjecture 1708.02370 solved 2023 2024 2025
notes: Conjecture 4 is proved for bounded-treedepth and bounded-pathwidth minor-closed classes (arXiv:2012.05554, 2022); the general case chi*(M_H) <= 2*td(H) - 2 remains open. Third internal reference (arXiv:2508.14332) is a false match unrelated to this conjecture.
